goal: Create a safe shared primitive for worker loops with cancellation, injectable backoff clock, test-bounded mode, explicit error handling, and no busy spin.

freshScanBefore.selectionReason: The repo already keeps reusable async primitives in src/lib/async. Reusing an existing source module avoids increasing the production source module count budget while keeping concrete worker behavior unchanged.

reasonSelected: Adds a shared worker-loop primitive beside existing async primitives.
Does not connect the primitive to queueWorker or any concrete worker in this wave.
Focused tests cover abort exit, test-bounded iterations, transient-error backoff, fatal error propagation, no tight loop, and stop condition behavior.
The focused suite includes existing worker boundary tests to confirm behavior was not changed.

before.sourceModuleBudgetImpact: initial new source file attempt raised source module count to 1302 and failed performance budget

after.noBusySpin: loop sleeps with at least MIN_WORKER_LOOP_BACKOFF_MS between continuing iterations
after.fatalErrorBehavior: throws by default or when onError returns throw
after.sourceModuleBudgetImpact: no production source module count increase after moving tests under tests/async and reusing the existing async source module

gateEvidence.focusedTests: npx jest tests/async/cancellableWorkerLoop.test.ts src/lib/async/mapWithConcurrencyLimit.test.ts src/workers/queueWorker.boundary.test.ts tests/workers/queueRunnerLifecycle.contract.test.ts tests/perf/performance-budget.test.ts --runInBand; 5 suites passed, 33 tests passed
gateEvidence.fullJestRunInBand: npm test -- --runInBand; 662 suites passed, 1 skipped, 3924 tests passed
gateEvidence.releaseVerifyPostPush: pending by design until the commit is pushed
